seoTitle: How to Integrate Slack With WhatsApp (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Connect WhatsApp to Slack using a marketplace bridge app so customer chats route into dedicated Slack channels automatically.
intro: Bridging customer-facing WhatsApp conversations into your internal Slack workspace helps teams respond faster without switching apps. This guide covers installing a WhatsApp connector from the Slack App Marketplace and routing chats into channels.

steps.heading: Open the Slack App Directory
steps.body: From your Slack workspace, access the Apps section to browse available marketplace connectors.

steps.heading: Choose a WhatsApp bridge app
steps.body: Search the directory for a certified connector, such as those built for WhatsApp Business integration, and select it.

steps.heading: Install the integration
steps.body: Click 'Add to Slack' to install the app, then review and approve the requested permission scopes.

steps.heading: Authenticate your WhatsApp number
steps.body: Follow the redirect to the connector's setup portal and scan the QR code using your WhatsApp app to link your number.

steps.heading: Route chats into a channel
steps.body: Map your WhatsApp threads to a specific Slack channel, then test by sending a message and replying from within Slack.

faq.question: Do I need WhatsApp Business API for this to work?
faq.answer: A standard WhatsApp number can work with simpler connectors, but higher-volume or enterprise use typically requires the WhatsApp Business API.

faq.question: Can I connect multiple WhatsApp numbers to one Slack workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, most connectors support mapping several numbers to different channels to avoid mixing conversations.

faq.question: Will replies from Slack show up correctly in WhatsApp?
faq.answer: Yes, properly configured connectors support bi-directional messaging so your reply appears as a normal WhatsApp message.

faq.question: Is this integration free?
faq.answer: Pricing varies by connector, with many offering a limited free tier and paid plans for higher message volumes.
